summary: DHH celebrates the first Rails book, 'Agile Web Development with Rails,' selling over 1,000 copies in its opening week despite being unfinished. He notes the success validates the model of releasing an early, unpolished version with a promise of a polished follow-up. He sees this as an encouraging sign for a paid content ecosystem around Rails and hints at other commercial content ideas he's been promoting. The post closes with optimism about Rails' growing momentum as it approaches version 1.0.
key_points: The first Rails book sold 1,000+ copies in its first week while still in beta/unfinished form
The early-release publishing model was broadly accepted by the community
DHH sees paid content (books and beyond) as important for the Rails ecosystem's health
Rails is gaining significant momentum heading toward its 1.0 release
money_quotes: It's great to see that releasing the unpolished version now, with the promise of a clean version later, was accepted so broadly.
It's also a great sign of encouragement for the ecosystem of paid content around Rails.
Rails is in stronger shape than ever and the race towards 1.0 is surely in.
key_insight: The rapid commercial success of the first Rails book—even in unfinished form—validated both the early-release publishing model and the growing demand for a paid content ecosystem around the framework.
